title: Ensure That the Log Metric Filter and Alerts Exist for Cloud  Storage IAM Permission Changes

input_row.title: Ensure That the Log Metric Filter and Alerts Exist for Cloud  Storage IAM Permission Changes
input_row.description: It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for Cloud Storage Bucket IAM changes.
input_row.rationale: Monitoring changes to cloud storage bucket permissions may reduce the time needed to detect and correct permissions on sensitive cloud storage buckets and objects inside the bucket.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console Ensure the prescribed log metric is present: 1. For each project that contains cloud storage buckets, go to Logging/Logs- based Metrics by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics. 2. In the User-defined Metrics section, ensure at least one metric is present with the filter text: resource.type="gcs_bucket" AND protoPayload.methodName="storage.setIamPermissions" Ensure that the prescribed alerting policy is present: 3. Go to Alerting by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/monitoring/alerting. 4. Under the Policies section, ensure that at least one alert policy exists for the log metric above. Clicking on the policy should show that it is configured with a condition. For example, Violates when: Any logging.googleapis.com/user/ stream is above a threshold of 0 for greater than 0 seconds means that the alert will trigger for any new owner change. Verify that the chosen alerting thresholds make sense for the user's organization. 5. Ensure that the appropriate notifications channels have been set up. From Google Cloud CLI Ensure that the prescribed log metric is present: 1. List the log metrics: gcloud logging metrics list --format json 2. Ensure that the output contains at least one metric with the filter set to: resource.type=gcs_bucket AND protoPayload.methodName="storage.setIamPermissions" 3. Note the value of the property metricDescriptor.type for the identified metric, in the format logging.googleapis.com/user/. Ensure the prescribed alerting policy is present: 4. List the alerting policies: gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json 5. Ensure that the output contains an least one alert policy where: • conditions.conditionThreshold.filter is set to metric.type=\"logging.googleapis.com/user/\" • AND enabled is set to true
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console Create the prescribed log metric: 1. Go to Logging/Logs-based Metrics by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics and click "CREATE METRIC". 2. Click the down arrow symbol on the Filter Bar at the rightmost corner and select Convert to Advanced Filter. 3. Clear any text and add: resource.type="gcs_bucket" AND protoPayload.methodName="storage.setIamPermissions" 4. Click Submit Filter. Display logs appear based on the filter text entered by the user. 5. In the Metric Editor menu on right, fill out the name field. Set Units to 1 (default) and Type to Counter. This ensures that the log metric counts the number of log entries matching the user's advanced logs query. 6. Click Create Metric. Create the prescribed Alert Policy: 1. Identify the newly created metric under the section User-defined Metrics at https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics. 2. Click the 3-dot icon in the rightmost column for the new metric and select Create alert from Metric. A new page appears. 3. Fill out the alert policy configuration and click Save. Choose the alerting threshold and configuration that makes sense for the user's organization. For example, a threshold of zero(0) for the most recent value will ensure that a notification is triggered for every owner change in the project: Set `Aggregator` to `Count` Set `Configuration`: - Condition: above - Threshold: 0 - For: most recent value 4. Configure the desired notifications channels in the section Notifications. 5. Name the policy and click Save. From Google Cloud CLI Create the prescribed Log Metric: • Use the command: gcloud beta logging metrics create Create the prescribed alert policy: • Use the command: gcloud alpha monitoring policies create
input_row.impact: Enabling of logging may result in your project being charged for the additional logs usage. These charges could be significant depending on the size of the organization.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit field is complete and includes specific commands in both the Google Cloud Console and Google Cloud CLI.
- GCP APIs can be queried for the necessary information related to log metrics and alerting policies.
- The rationale, audit, and remediation sections indicate this is a straightforward check for metrics and policies that can be automated.
- The use of gcloud commands supports that an automated approach is suitable.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_logging_metric_alert_policy_cloud_storage_iam_changes

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
The automated program would check the following:

1. **Log Metrics Verification:**
   - Use Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) command: 
     ```bash
     gcloud logging metrics list --format json
     ```
   - Check that the output contains at least one metric with the filter that matches:
     - `resource.type=gcs_bucket AND protoPayload.methodName="storage.setIamPermissions"`
   - Pass/Fail criteria: Check if there is at least one entry that has a `metricDescriptor.type` following the format `logging.googleapis.com/user/<metric_name>`.

2. **Alert Policies Verification:**
   - Use Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) command:
     ```bash
     gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json
     ```
   - Confirm that the output includes at least one alert policy meeting the following conditions:
     - `conditions.conditionThreshold.filter` should be set to:
       - `metric.type="logging.googleapis.com/user/<metric_name>"`
     - The policy must be `enabled`.
   - Pass/Fail criteria: There should be at least one alert policy verifying the specified filter and that it is enabled.

This will ensure compliance with the control regarding the existence of log metrics and alert policies for Cloud Storage IAM permission changes.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The task of verifying the existence of log metrics and alert policies can be efficiently automated using the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) and GCP APIs. The commands provided allow for programmatic checks of the necessary configurations, which aligns with the capabilities of the GCP CLI.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- The automation details are mostly accurate, but there are minor improvements that could be made for clarity and completeness.
- The `gcloud logging metrics list --format json` command is valid and correctly used to list log metrics.
- The `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json` command is also valid, but note that it uses an alpha command, which might have limitations or changes in the future. It would be beneficial to check if there is a stable or beta version available.
- The program name `gcp_logging_metric_alert_policy_cloud_storage_iam_changes` is descriptive but could be more concise. It does follow the convention of including the service and resource.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

1. **Command Stability:** Consider using stable or beta versions of the `gcloud` commands if available, as alpha commands can be subject to change.
   
2. **Program Name:** A more concise name could be `gcp_logging_iam_changes_alerts`. This still conveys the purpose but is shorter and easier to reference.

3. **Additional Checks:** Ensure that the metric and alert policy are not only present but also correctly configured to trigger alerts under the desired conditions. This might involve checking for specific threshold values or notification channels.

4. **Documentation:** Include a note on how to handle scenarios where multiple metrics or policies exist, ensuring that the correct ones are evaluated.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-structured and mostly accurate, with only minor improvements suggested. The use of GCP CLI and APIs is appropriate for the task.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify

- Implement the suggested improvements, particularly regarding the use of stable or beta commands if available, and consider refining the program name for clarity.
- Ensure additional checks for configuration correctness are included in the automated script.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:** Both AI opinions agree that the task of verifying the existence of log metrics and alert policies for Cloud Storage IAM permission changes can be automated using the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) and GCP APIs. The commands provided allow for programmatic checks of the necessary configurations, which aligns with the capabilities of the GCP CLI. The use of stable or beta versions of the gcloud commands ensures reliability and accuracy in the automation process.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
gcp_logging_metric_alert_policy_cloud_storage_iam_changes

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- GCP Resource/Service: Cloud Storage Buckets, Logging, Monitoring
- API/CLI Method: 
  - `gcloud logging metrics list --format json`
  - `gcloud monitoring policies list --format json`
- Properties to Check:
  - Log Metrics: Ensure presence of a metric with filter `resource.type="gcs_bucket" AND protoPayload.methodName="storage.setIamPermissions"`
  - Alert Policies: Confirm alert policies include conditions with `metric.type="logging.googleapis.com/user/<metric_name>"`
- Pass Criteria: At least one log metric and alert policy meeting the specified conditions are present.
- Fail Criteria: Absence of the required log metric or alert policy.

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:** There were no disagreements between the AI opinions; both concurred on the automated approach, and the second opinion provided additional insights on command stability and naming conventions.

**SOURCE:** CIS_Google_Cloud_Platform_Foundation_Benchmark_v4_0_0
